title: GoOutLiving
author: Jonathan Brandt
description: A cancer survivors’ journey that inspires a health and wellness community that supports each other through education and the attitude to "GoOutLiving"! Come join the GoOutLiving Community on Skool at https://www.skool.com/gooutliving and learn how to live a healthy lifestyle.

title: Keep Pointing Up Podcast
author: Rhea Media Group
description: We point men to Christ, across Acadiana, regardless of where they sit on Sunday. We gather monthly for a meal and a message to encourage community and brotherhood.

title: The Concrete Blueprint
description:
The Concrete Blueprint is a concrete education podcast from Quick Concrete Solutions for homeowners, HOA leaders, builders, and property managers who want straight answers about driveway replacement, patio installation, sidewalk replacement, concrete repair, concrete tear-out and replacement, and long-lasting concrete construction in Iowa.
If you want expert insight on concrete driveways, concrete patios, driveway tear-out and replacement, concrete finishes, sealing, reinforcement, rebar, mix design, and concrete best practices, this podcast is built for you.

title: Emani Sings Interview
author: Music In the City HTX
description: Exclusive interview with R&B and Soul Artist Emani Sings

title: The Justin Thomas Show
author: Volkayto Original Audio Series
description:
The Justin Thomas Show is a Volkayto Original Audio Series produced by Volkayto Media, featuring exclusive interviews and compelling conversations with influential voices across entertainment, film, television, music, sports, business, and culture. Hosted by Justin Thomas, the series is entirely unscripted, uncensored, and improvised, allowing every conversation to unfold naturally and authentically. Each episode also features JT’s signature JT Rants, delivering candid commentary on current events, culture, mental health, and everyday life.


Originally debuting in 2011 as a live, interactive internet talk show streamed on Ustream, the series connected audiences through live call-ins and real-time conversations. Following the conclusion of its live broadcast run, the show transitioned into an on-demand audio series, expanding its reach to listeners worldwide. It was relaunched in 2014, and entered its third major iteration (2026), reimagined with cinematic production, elevated storytelling, and a renewed focus on premium long-form interviews and original programming.


The series succeeds On Air With JT (2010–2025), the acclaimed independent audio series produced by Volkayto Media. Across more than 2,000 episodes over fifteen years, On Air With JT became known for its improvisational format, signature JT Rants, comedy, and exclusive interviews with Grammy, Emmy, and Oscar winners, along with leading figures in Hollywood, music, sports, entrepreneurship, and the arts.


Since 2010, the series has championed mental health awareness through Justin Thomas’s candid personal storytelling, earning recognition for its authenticity, thoughtful preparation, and meaningful dialogue. In March 2023, Thomas conducted nine interviews in eight days, underscoring the series’ commitment to preparation, endurance, and meaningful conversation.


Season One of the third major iteration (2026) features exclusive conversations with Emmy Award-winning actress and comedian Kym Whitley and Emmy Award-winning, NAACP Image Award-winning filmmaker and cultural storyteller Coodie Simmons, continuing the series’ tradition of spotlighting influential voices through authentic, unscripted dialogue.



Today, The Justin Thomas Show carries forward a legacy spanning more than 17 years and over 3,000 produced episodes, ushering in a bold new era of premium audio storytelling. Every episode is entirely unscripted, uncensored, and fully improvised, allowing authentic conversations to unfold naturally, where genuine human connection takes precedence over scripted interviews. Through exclusive interviews, compelling storytelling, and the return of JT Rants, The Justin Thomas Show continues its mission to inform, entertain, challenge perspectives, and inspire audiences around the world.


Volkayto Original Audio Series 
Produced by Volkayto Media
OnAirWithJT.Com

Become a supporter of this podcast: https://www.spreaker.com/podcast/the-justin-thomas-show--7065040/support.

title: TradeOps Radio
description:
TradeOps Radio is a podcast for home service business owners who want to grow their business the right way.
From HVAC to plumbing, electrical, roofing, and other home service trades, we talk about the real strategies behind building profitable service businesses. That includes marketing, operations, sales, leadership, and everything in between.
Expect honest conversations, real-world insights, and practical lessons you can actually use in your business.

title: Beyond Biology
author: Siddharth Sundar: Beyond Biology
description: Evolution doesn't just create life—it experiments with it. Beyond Biology explores the molecular stories behind nature's most extraordinary adaptations.

title: The San Clemente Podcast
author: The San Clemente Podcast
description: Bringing you culture, lifestyle, sports and politics from the best place in the world to live in.

title: Under The Owl Tree
author: Grey Druid
description:
"Under The Owl Tree" a podcast for those who have felt a connection with something beyond. 
My name is Daniel and I am an experiencer who speaks with other experiencers who have walked through these encounters and work to learn and grow from them. We will share stories of encounters with extraterrestrial intelligences, dimensional travelers, and non-human beings that exist outside our perception or physical dimension.

title: The Perfect Odyssey Podcast
author: Amalia Henry
description: Conversations about identity, confidence, nervous system regulation, entrepreneurship, and building a life and business that aligns with who you're becoming.

title: Split Streams
author: The Badger's Apprentice
description:
Welcome to SPLIT STREAMS, a monthly short story anthology where two writers are presented with the same starting point - a prompt, a scene, an idea - and must write what-ever story they deem fit, discovering the extremes of each-others imaginations, and the commonalities between them. With each episode written by one writer, and narrated by the other (and/or a guest.)


The same starting point could walk you through a man’s pain, or transport you to the depths of space on the edge of human understanding. It could lead you thought the fear, secrecy and social manufacturings of a political uprising, or drag you through the depths of a hell from which there is no escapes. 


SPLIT STREAMS is written and presented by the talented REBECCA MILES HENSON, audiobook narrator and published author of THE CRACKS WE SEE THROUGH, and many short stories and poems. And JEFF GRAY, serial procrastinator, forever working on his first novel while getting distracted by his film based youtube channel THE BADGERS APPRENTICE.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: I’ve Got A Story For You
author: Luke and Josh
description: Join your hosts, Luke and Josh, as they take listeners on a hilarious journey through their past escapades, absurd mishaps, and the unforgettable moments that have defined their friendship over the years. Each episode will blend storytelling, dark humour, featuring hilarious tales from their adventures, whether they happened during a simple night out, a date, or on holiday.

title: Above the Fold
author: Cincinnati Business Courier
description: *ON HIATUS* Business news shouldn't be boring. Above the Fold is a weekly podcast by the Cincinnati Business Courier, bringing you the week's top news and interviews with the biggest names in Cincinnati.

title: 100proof
author: Prove
description:
Welcome to the trust and identity happy hour show. 
In this industry, we do some of our best relationship building and idea sharing when we gather for happy hours. We all know that’s where the real conversations are happening. Co-hosts Sage Avarda and Steve Craig are bottling up that energy and delivering it to you, no matter where you are, in this monthly series!
Each episode, Sage and Steve will sit down with a leader in the trust and identity space to enjoy a rarified spirit or wine while chatting through all things verification and authentication.
Cheers!

title: Dear Curious Soul
author: Duschanca
description:
Dear Curious Soul is a podcast for those who feel called to slow down, soften, and look within.

Hosted by Duschanca, this podcast isn’t here to give you answers — it’s here to invite curiosity. Curiosity about healing, growth, self-discovery, and the quiet wisdom that already lives inside you.

Each episode is an invitation to explore new perspectives, question old beliefs, and reconnect with yourself in a gentler, more open way. Some conversations may spark something deep within you. Others may simply plant a seed.

Whether you’re navigating healing, personal growth, or a season of change, Dear Curious Soul offers space to wonder, reflect, and ask the questions only you can answer.

Listen with an open mind, a kind heart — and your dear, curious soul.

title: Everett Silvertips Fireside Chat
author: Everett Silvertips
description:
Enjoy a cozy conversation with players and hear all about what is going on in the Silvertips next to a blazing fire thanks to our sponsors of Anthony's Woodfire Grill.

Production support provided by the Snohomish Podcast Network.

title: Witness Protection Blues
description: People living under assumed identities in the witness protection program and the reality of abandoning your entire life to testify against criminals. Explores the isolation, constant fear, and identity loss that comes with trading your name for safety. Documents both the people who disappeared into new lives and those who violated protection rules with fatal consequences.

title: Web of Shadows Tracing Criminal Intricacies
description: Join us as we traverse through the labyrinthine networks of crime and justice, exploring the intricate patterns of criminal behavior as we delve deep into the murky underworld of urban environments. This podcast uncovers the hidden mechanisms that drive crime, utilizing psychological insights, expert interviews, and detailed case analyses to expose the climate of fear and systemic failures that allow such darkness to persist. We'll bring you onto the scene of the crime, provide you with the tools to understand the criminal mind, and challenge your perception of justice.

title: Vortex of Vice Unearthing City Serpents
description: Vortex of Vice plunges into the underground worlds of urban life, forensic investigations, and complex criminal psychology. A comprehensive probe into the diverse intricacies of the criminal mind and law enforcement tactics, our detailed case analysis and expert interviews provide a unique perspective on crime in our cities. Together, we'll delve into the unsettling realities that shape our understanding of justice.

title: Fever Insider
author: Indy Star
description: Join IndyStar Fever insider Chloe Peterson and reporter Brian Haenchen as they take you inside the Indiana Fever with insights and analysis from games, front office decisions and go behind the scenes with special guests from the team to around the WNBA for direct access to all things Fever.

title: Vice Squad
description: Modern prostitution enforcement from both sides—sex workers navigating dangerous conditions and vice detectives targeting trafficking while wrestling with the complexities of consensual sex work. Features honest conversations about an underground economy that exists despite criminalization. Explores the gap between prostitution laws and street-level reality.

title: Urban Shadows Decoding Invisible Crime Trails
description: Urban Shadows takes listeners on a deep dive into the unseen trails of urban crime. Through detailed crime scene analysis and discussions with forensic investigators, we shed light on the invisible clues that are often overlooked during criminal investigations. The pursuit for justice commences in the shadows.

title: Unlocking Urban Mysteries Examining Criminal Psychology
description: Find revelation in secret corners of the urban landscape, examining the criminal mind. Figure out what shapes crime and justice in the city with expert insight.

title: Kuomba Pamoja
author: Kuomba Pamoja
description:
Welcome to the official podcast of Kuomba Pamoja Church, a vibrant multi-cultural community connected to the United Methodist Church (UMC).
The name "Kuomba Pamoja" comes from the Swahili words for "Praying Together" or "Worshiping Together." Our heart is to unite believers from all backgrounds in prayer and the Word. Join us as we worship together through powerful messages preached in both English and Swahili.
Whether you are looking for spiritual encouragement or a deeper connection to Christ, we invite you to join our global family as we seek the Lord as one.
Karibuni sana — Everyone is wel

title: Underworld Unveiled: Peeling Back Urban Darkness
description: Step into the underbelly of urban life on 'Underworld Unveiled: Peeling Back Urban Darkness'. We dissect the complexities of crime, dive into criminal psychology, and pull back the curtains on the concrete jungle. From deep case analysis to interviews with law enforcement experts, forensic specialists, and criminal justice professionals, we unravel the various layers of criminal behavior unravelled, shedding light on the pursuit of justice. This show uses AI technology to create engaging audio content.

title: PojavLauncher APK Complete Installation
description: Learn how to install and use PojavLauncher APK to play Minecraft Java Edition on Android. This episode covers installation, features, performance tips, and troubleshooting. More guides: https://pojavvlauncherapk.net/

title: CEI Insights
author: CEI
description: CEI is an AI engineering and transformation partner that designs, builds, and operationalizes production grade AI across enterprise systems. We help organizations move from ambition to execution by embedding AI into mission critical platforms, enterprise data environments, and operational workflows.

title: HR’s Worst Nightmare: Podcast
author: HR’s Worst Nightmare
description:
HR’s Worst Nightmare is the chaotic, unfiltered podcast hosted by Graeson & Verity; packed with celebrity interviews, gossip, chaotic guests, internet drama, pranks, games, bad takes and absolutely zero HR approval. 
Expect glam energy, ridiculous stories, unhinged debates and conversations that probably should’ve stayed in the group chat.
🎧 New episodes weekly on Spotify, Apple Podcasts, YouTube & iHeartRadio

title: Underground Frequencies Mapping Crime Signals
description: An exploration in the hidden wavelengths of crime, 'Underground Frequencies' goes beyond the visible spectrum to observe the unseen signals that communicate the truth behind criminal behaviors. With commentary from forensic audiology experts and criminal analysts, we tap into the very pulses that express the essence of urban crime.

title: Twisted Networks Decrypting Criminal Underworld
description: Descend into the intricate labyrinth of urban crime, expertly navigated for your understanding. This podcast explores captivating incidents, the minds that architect them, and the relentless pursuit of justice in the face of indomitable odds. Prepare for a deep dive into the dark corners of city life.

title: Backlink Providers
description:
Backlink Providers, your trusted partner for strategic link building and off-page SEO solutions. We help businesses improve their online visibility, strengthen domain authority, and achieve higher search engine rankings through ethical and effective SEO practices.

Our expertise includes link building, off-page SEO, press release distribution, content marketing, and premium backlink services designed to deliver long-term organic growth. We focus on securing high-quality, niche-relevant backlinks from authoritative websites that enhance credibility and drive targeted traffic to your business.

At Backlink Providers, we understand that every business has unique goals. That's why we create customized SEO strategies tailored to your industry, competition, and growth objectives. Our commitment to quality, transparency, and measurable results has made us a reliable partner for businesses looking to build a stronger digital presence.

Whether you're a startup, local business, or established brand, we're here to help you grow your authority and dominate search results.

title: Ope, it's Murder
author: Lindsey and Libbie
description:
Midwest roots. Dark stories. Strong coffee.

Two best friends uncovering Wisconsin and Midwest true crime, one cup at a time.

title: Just Knowing Tomorrow
author: Isiah M.
description:
Just Knowing Tomorrow is where today's headlines collide with yesterday's myths and tomorrow's possibilities.

Every episode dives into world news, ancient mythology, conspiracy theories, unexplained phenomena, science, philosophy, and the strange ideas that keep curious minds awake at night. From global events to forgotten legends, hidden histories to futuristic speculation, nothing is off-limits if it challenges the way we see reality.

Wrapped in a surreal, cinematic atmosphere with a healthy dose of nerd culture, Just Knowing Tomorrow treats every discussion like another piece of a massive puzzle waiting to be solved. Whether we're connecting mythology to modern society, examining the latest world events, exploring bizarre internet mysteries, or dissecting your favorite fictional universes, curiosity always comes first.

If you've ever wondered, "What if the world is stranger than it looks?"- you're in the right place.

Question everything. Learn relentlessly. Just keep knowing tomorrow.

title: Traveling Light with Stephanie Mirza
author: WVPE 88.1 Public Radio
description:
*This week on Traveling Light with Stephanie Mirza...*
Stephanie and her husband Mahan will spend the next two months traveling Europe with only their carry-on bags! Join us as we start our Journey in Chicago, explore Bosnia, Italy, Amsterdam and beyond!

title: The Takedown
description: Major organized crime busts from initial investigation through dramatic raids and arrests. Each episode follows one multi-year operation targeting drug cartels, mafia families, or international criminal networks. Features agents, prosecutors, and informants explaining how years of surveillance and infiltration lead to coordinated takedowns that dismantle entire criminal enterprises.

title: The Snitch
description: Criminal informants who betrayed their associates to avoid prison time or seek revenge—and the complicated morality of cooperation. Features CI handlers, prosecutors, and sometimes the informants themselves explaining how snitching works and what happens to people who cross that line. Explores whether informants are heroes helping justice or traitors enabling their own crimes.

title: The Ponzi Unraveling
description: Investment frauds that collapsed catastrophically, destroying savings and lives while enriching charismatic con artists. Each episode traces one Ponzi scheme from inception through spectacular failure, examining how smart people believed impossible returns and why fraud persists despite countless examples. Features victims, investigators, and occasionally the fraudsters themselves explaining their crimes.

title: The Money Launderers
description: Following the complex schemes that transform dirty money into legitimate wealth—from shell companies to art auctions to cryptocurrency mixers. Features former money launderers, financial investigators, and forensic accountants explaining how billions flow through the global economy's shadows. Reveals the infrastructure that makes organized crime profitable and (mostly) invisible.

title: Live beneath ur means
description:
Living beneath your means is one of the smartest financial habits you can develop. It means spending less than you earn, avoiding unnecessary debt, and making thoughtful choices with your money. Instead of trying to impress others with expensive purchases, you focus on saving, investing, and preparing for the future.

This lifestyle brings financial freedom, reduces stress, and helps you reach important goals such as buying a home, starting a business, or building an emergency fund. Living beneath your means is not about depriving yourself—it's about making wise decisions today so you can enjoy greater security and opportunities tomorrow.

Every small step, from creating a budget to avoiding impulse purchases, can lead to long-term financial success. Remember, true wealth is built by discipline, patience, and consistent smart money habits.

title: The Kingpin's Fall
description: Criminal empires collapsing under law enforcement pressure, internal betrayal, or their own arrogance. Profiles drug lords, crime bosses, and organized crime leaders who seemed untouchable until everything unraveled. Examines the investigations that brought them down and what happened to their organizations after their arrests.

title: Conversations With Sylvia
author: Waindim Nkumi
description: This podcast is a mind travel through Bamenda Cameroon with cultural discoveries that leave u impressed and societal issues that cut across nationally and globally.

title: The Justice Weavers: Interlacing Threads of Crime & Punishment
description: An in-depth exploration of true crime scenarios, criminal psychology, and the gritty reality of urban life. 'The Justice Weavers' interlaces complex threads of lawbreaking and law enforcement, presenting compelling stories of crime and punishment. Featuring detailed case studies, insightful interviews with crime experts, and a riveting outlook on the pursuit of justice. This show exploring the future of audio through AI-generated content.

title: The Forgers
description: Master counterfeiters of art, documents, currency, and identities—and the investigators who hunt them. Each episode profiles one sophisticated forgery operation, from fake Picassos fooling museums to counterfeit passports enabling international crime. Reveals the technical brilliance required to deceive experts and the detective work that exposes even perfect fakes.

title: Niels Klim's Journey under the Ground
description: Niels Klim's Underground Travels, originally published in Latin as "Nicolai Klimii Iter Subterraneum" (1741) is a satirical science-fiction/fantasy novel written by Ludvig Holberg, a Norwegian-Danish dramatist, historian, and essayist, born in Bergen, Norway. It was his first and only novel. It describes a utopian society from an outsider's point of view, and often pokes fun at diverse cultural and social topics such as moral, science, sexual equality, religion, governments, and philosophy. (Summary by Wikipedia)

title: New Bodies for Old
description: Maurice Renard's little known but delightfully bizarre tale of mad science run amok owes much to H. G. Wells' The Island of Dr. Moreau while also forging its own path by taking Wells' plot as a starting point and pushing it to ridiculous extremes. When a young man named Nicholas goes to visit his beloved scientist uncle Dr. Lerne in a remote French chateau, he is immediately put on his guard by his uncle's strange behavior, the mysterious Germans who now work with his uncle in a secret laboratory on the premises, on the strange noises he hears in the night... But Nicholas' desire to discover what his uncle's secret experiments are truly all about coupled with his strong desire for the young woman Emma who is staying with his uncle may soon find Nicholas in a greater peril that he ever possibly could have dreamed. - Summary by Ben Tucker

title: Lucian's True History
description: One of the earliest works of science-fiction (nearly 2,000 years old). It has space travel (including war in space), lunar civilization, and aliens, along with more fantasy elements, such as the afterlife and Greek gods. A satire on contemporary tall tales. - Summary by Terry Kroenung

title: At-homish
description: Join Wil Johnson, Chief Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion Officer for Snohomish County, as he helps make you feel At-homish. Whether you are in your home town or discovering a new one, there are ways we can help everyone belong and feel at-home. Wil breaks down the different perspectives and strategies on how to make everyone in your community feel like they belong. Come on in and make yourself At-homish.

title: The Fixer
description: Professional cleaners, PR crisis managers, and damage control specialists who make scandals disappear for the wealthy and powerful. Each episode reveals the elaborate cover-ups, evidence disposal, and reputation rehabilitation they orchestrate behind closed doors. Explores the moral compromises of being paid to hide truth and protect the guilty.

title: Unthinkable. A True Crime Podcast
author: Lisa
description: A true crime podcast which looks at the unthinkable neglect and abuse of the most vulnerable in our society. I will be releasing episodes once a week to start with.

title: Read the Room with Rich
author: Rich Patterson
description: Welcome to Read the Room with Rich

title: The Blackmail Files
description: Real extortion cases where victims were threatened with exposure of secrets, from political scandals to corporate fraud to personal indiscretions. Examines the psychology of blackmailers, the impossible choices victims face, and how some fought back while others paid forever. Reveals how extortion operates as an invisible crime that destroys lives in silence.

title: The Auction Block
description: Illegal markets where everything from stolen antiquities to human trafficking to endangered species gets bought and sold. Investigative journalists and law enforcement expose the auction houses, online platforms, and private sales that facilitate criminal commerce. Reveals how legitimate marketplaces become venues for laundering stolen goods and trafficking contraband.

title: Mortgage Talks
author: MortgageTalks
description: Join us for the best tips, do's, and don'ts in today's real estate/mortgage market. Listen to Informative interviews with industry professionals who only want the best for old and new homebuyers. Mortgage Talks is presented to you by FM Home Loans, LLC.

title: The Official Grindin Radio Podcast
author: DJ D-Lyfe
description:
Grindin Radio delivers the sound of the streets and the pulse of the culture. Stream DJ-driven performances, curated playlists, and high-energy mixes from today’s top DJs, plus entertainment news and trending culture you actually care about. Whether you’re working, driving, or turning up, Grindin Radio keeps you locked in.
🔥 Real DJs. Real energy. Real grind.

title: The Tingle Zone with Andrew Miller
author: Andrew Miller
description:
Hello, I'm Andrew Miller from Business Enjoyment, where success is about so much more than just money. In fact, I want you to enjoy your business so much 
‘it makes your bits tingle’. 
In these episodes, I’ll be interviewing successful entrepreneurs and people in business to uncover their personal journeys, their business tips and why they get a buzz out of what they do. 
In other words, what makes their bits tingle?
For more information on Business Enjoyment go to
www.businessenjoyment.com

title: The Arsonist's Signature
description: Fire investigators solving suspicious blazes and the twisted psychology of serial arsonists who torch buildings for profit, revenge, or compulsion. Each episode reconstructs one arson investigation, from the first spark to the courtroom, revealing how fire science catches criminals who think flames destroy all evidence. Explores why people burn things and how investigators read messages in ashes.

title: The GEO Shortlist
author: Periscopify
description:
When a buyer asks an AI which brand to trust, it answers with a few names. Three, maybe five. Your brand is one of them, or it isn't.

The GEO Shortlist is a show about how AI decides which brands it recommends, and which ones it only mentions. Host Caroline B. Klette talks to the CMOs living it and the experts studying it, digging into what puts a brand on the list, and what leaves it off.

Each episode looks at how AI models describe brands, how they form preferences, and how those quiet judgments shape buying decisions long before anyone talks to a salesperson.

You're visible. The question is whether you're chosen.

Follow so you don't miss the next one.

title: Misgivings
author: Kindle Project
description:
Misgivings breaks down what’s wrong with mainstream philanthropy and uplifts bold solutions. Through candid conversations filled with humor and heart, hosts Arianne Shaffer and Sadaf Rassoul Cameron connect with visionary change-makers rising to the call of this urgent moment and reshaping how money, power, and trust flow in a sector long overdue for change.


This show is for people working in philanthropy, donors sick of the old ways of doing things, everyone in the field looking for new ideas for how to move resources and share power and have greater impact.

title: Tales of the Darkened City Exposing Urban Crime
description: Engage in the tempestuous narrative surrounding urban crime. See how crime compares and contrasts across the city, curated with insights from experts in the field.

title: Interesting Facts about Everything
author: Dylan
description: To keep your mind curious, catch up on the interesting facts I'll discuss on every episode!

title: Rhoda And Jesse Make A Podcast
author: Rhodaandjessemakeapodcast
description: Sit back and relax.  Rhoda and Jesse are here with some hot takes on all their favorite movies, songs, artists, and much much more

title: Tech News Podcast
description: Tech News Podcast is an editorial technology publication built for readers and listeners who want more than headlines. We cover innovation with context, clarity and perspective, connecting the technical story to its human, business, legal and social consequences.

title: Subway Shadows: The Underground Crime Chronicles
description: Subway Shadows dives into the hidden criminal world that thrives in the bowels of the city's underbelly. This investigative storytelling podcast uncovers the darkest corners of urban crime, focusing on the criminal psychology that emerges in transit systems. Our hosts interview law enforcement experts, forensic specialists, and criminal justice professionals, shedding light not only on notorious cases but also on the lesser-known crimes concealed beneath the hustle and bustle of the city.

title: Subterranean Secrets Probing the Urban Abyss
description: Subterranean Secrets goes beneath the surface to expose the hidden horrors of metropolitan crime. Featuring expert analysis of criminal psychology and deep dives into real-life cases, it offers listeners an unfiltered exploration of the darker side of urban life. Through interviews with law enforcement specialists, forensic experts, and criminal justice professionals, we endeavor to provide insights into the criminal mind and the relentless pursuit of justice. Thrilling, heartbreaking, and at times terrifying, Subterranean Secrets dives deep to bring the dark recesses of crime to light.

title: Alien 2 Me
author: Haunted Mitten
description: A podcast about Ufology, as told by someone who knows nothing about it

title: Sketchy Silhouettes Narrating Urban Crime Tales
description: Push beyond the glaring city lights into the murky shadows of a cryptic underworld. This podcast melds riveting urban crime narratives with thought-provoking insights into criminal psychology, pieced together with the tireless efforts of justice seekers within the urban cauldron.

title: Sirens in Shadows Navigating Urban Justice
description: Explore crime's underbelly, just beneath the bustling cities we inhabit. Experience the chilling narrative of urban crime, the fascinating psychology behind it, and the relentless pursuit of justice in layered cityscapes. Every episode unveils new mysteries, deeper insights, and a fresh perspective on urban justice.

title: Crush Your Social
description:
Hosted by Antonia Semanske, founder of Crush Your Social, this podcast helps service-based business owners turn their online presence into a clear extension of their expertise.

If your business runs on referrals, reputation, and results—but your content isn’t reflecting that—this show is for you.

You’ll learn how to use platforms like Instagram and LinkedIn to build visibility, reinforce trust, and position yourself as the go-to in your space—without burning time or following trends that don’t serve your goals.

Practical insights. Strategic content. Credibility that converts.

title: Sinister Metropolis: Dissecting Dark Deeds
description: Delve into the intricate intricacies of urban crime in 'Sinister Metropolis: Dissecting Dark Deeds.' Each episode retraces the steps leading up to notorious criminal acts, presents an analytical perspective into the mindset of the perpetrators, and shares firsthand accounts from forensic experts and law enforcers. We strip away the sensationalism, focusing on facts and psychological insights to understand the darkest corners of our cities.

title: The High School Dirt
author: Arsene
description: High school wasn't just about academics 😭 it was about survival and survival meant everything to some of us 👀 welcome where we dig in and talk about the dirt and marks that high school left in some of us

title: Sinister Boulevards Tearing Urban Crime Veil
description: Dive deep beneath the surface of metropolitan life, into a realm where truth and justice are more than black and white. This podcast explores the riveting world of urban crime, the complexities of criminal psychology, and the ever-challenging pursuit of justice. Expect real-life case examinations, expert analysis, and the untold stories 'behind the blue'.

title: Sidewalk Noir: Decoding the Urban Abyss
description: Sidewalk Noir is a podcast that plunges listeners deep into the grim reality of urban living, the city's unseen labyrinth of crime, and the fight for justice. Join the probe into criminal psychology, law enforcement challenges, and true crime stories that paint a chilling picture of life in the city shadows. The podcast brings to light the lived experiences of those who have been touched by the sinister side of urban living - victims, detectives, forensic experts, and even the criminals themselves.

title: Riddles of the Nightfall Deciphering Crime Enigmas
description: Riddles of the Nightfall dives into the perplexing puzzles that have eluded criminal justice professionals. Offering a fresh perspective on famous cases, we dissect crime conundrums with experts to understand what makes these mysteries remain unresolved. Darkness conceals more than it reveals, it's time to uncover.

title: Marvels of Machinery in Space (Ed Reads Short Sci-fi, vol. X)
description: Thirteen science fiction short stories. - Summary by The Reader

title: Noir by Nightfall Decoding Urban Shadows
description: Venture into the eerie unknown of dark urban tales and the personas of criminal worlds. This podcast delves into the gritty mysteries of criminal psychology and urban crime, told by those in law enforcement and criminal justice. Explore definitive moments, investigative twists, and the endless pursuit of justice that keeps cities vigil.

title: Short Science Fiction Collection 061
description: Science fiction is a genre encompassing imaginative works that take place in this world or that of the author’s creation where anything is possible. The only rules are those set forth by the author. The speculative nature of the genre inspires thought and plants seeds that have led to advances in science. The genre can spark an interest in the sciences and is cited as the impetus for the career choice of many scientists. It is a playing field to explore social perspectives, predictions of the future, and engage in adventures unbound into the richness of the human mind. - Summary by A. Gramour Note: Section 16, In The Year 2889 was originally published under the name of Jules Verne, but is currently believed to be the work of his son, Michel Verne.

title: The DJ Sessions
description:
“The DJ Sessions” is a live streamed/podcast series featuring the hottest electronic music DJs with live mixes and interviews streamed live to a global audience.

“The DJ Sessions” DistributionThe series constantly places in the “Top Ten” on Twitch Music and the “Top Five” in the “Electronic Music”, “DJ”, “Dance Music” categories. TDJS is rated in the Top 0.12% of live streaming shows on Twitch out of millions of live streamers.

“The DJ Sessions” is listed in the Feedspot directory as one of the Top 60 Podcasts on the Web.The series has also been mentioned in some of the top electronic publications for interviews in Magnetic Magazine, EDMTunes, MI4L, Vents Magazine, and Fame Magazine, and many more. 

TDJS was also listed in the “2022 Ultimate Directory of Podcasters” in the Music category. It has also been recognized by Apple twice as a “New and Noteworthy” podcast and featured three times in the Apple Music Store video podcast section.

UStream and Livestream have also listed the series as a “Featured” stream on their platforms since its inception.The series is also streamed live to multiple other platforms and hosted on several podcast sites.

It has a combined live streaming/podcast audience is over 125,000 viewers per week.

About The DJ Sessions Event Services

TDJSES is a 501c3 Non-profit charitable organization that’s main purpose is to provide music, art, fashion, dance, and entertainment to local and regional communities via events and video production programming distributed online for live and archival viewing.

title: Dangerous Liaisons
author: Dangerous Music
description: Dangerous Liaisons is a long-form interview podcast hosted by marek stycos — deep conversations with the producers, mixers, and engineers behind the records you love. Jack Joseph Puig, Sylvia Massy, Luca Pretolesi, Matt Wallace, and many more open up about their craft, process, and what it really takes to make music that stands the test of time. Presented by Dangerous Music.

title: Nocturne Chronicles: The Nightfall Justice
description: The 'Nocturne Chronicles' podcast enthralls listeners with its deep dive into the mysteries of dark urban life. This podcast explores the fascinating interplay between criminal psychology and justice, featuring detailed case analysis, expert interviews, and gripping, eerie narratives.

title: Battles for the Stars (Ed Reads Short Sci-fi, vol. III)
description: I: ‘The Sling and the Stone’ by Michael Shaara An obsessed Soviet commander has come up with the most devastating weapon the world has ever seen. What of the scientist who must put the order to destroy the USA into practice? II: ‘Slaughter on Dornell IV’ by Ivar Jorgensen One of the galaxy's greatest professional fighters. But is his agent so sly as to pit him against an opponent that could kill him? III: ‘The Hour of Battle’ by Robert Sheckley Waiting for the mind killers to strike in the depths of deep space! What's to be done? IV: ‘Defense Mech’ by Ray Bradbury The soldier's going mad! There's no time for ethics; tell him his home on Earth and throw him into battle. The result? Let Ray Bradbury tell you. V: ‘The Invisible Enemy’ by Arnold Castle Is sending the world's teenagers out to the grim battlefields truly the answer to achieve peace? VI: ‘Navy Day’ by Harry Harrison Futuristic machines provide intriguing end results. VII: ‘Belly Laugh’ by Ivar Jorgensen Futuristic machines provide embarrassing end results. VIII: ‘Hunter Patrol’ by Henry Beam Piper and John J. Mcguire ‘They just voted him into power. And the world into slavery. It is almost blasphemous to say anything against him. He has deprived all of us of our political and other liberties’. With the great mission complete, Benson returns to the battlefield. Naturally, the war concludes, and Benson enters into business, coming upon a method of ending all war. But time itself might have something to say about it... IX: ‘Bridge Crossing’ by Dave Dryfoos Robbie must choose loyalty to robot or to man in this dystopian tale, featuring a perilous chase across the Golden Gate Bridge. X: ‘Keep Your Shape’ by Robert Sheckley The ship approaches, a vanguard to an invasion! The problem? The scout party lacks discipline! They can’t…keep their shape. XI: ‘Project Hush’ by William Tenn A secret mission - so very secret - the first people on the moon! (yes, this story is that old) - but what will they find there? XII: (formerly 1 - file/source to be replaced: ‘The Sling and the Stone’ by Michael Shaara https://www.gutenberg.org/file... (Summary by The Reader)

title: Neighborhood Watch
description: Suburban communities harboring dark secrets—from hidden drug operations to domestic violence to neighbors who turned out to be fugitives or criminals. Explores how crimes hide in plain sight behind picket fences and how communities react when they discover monsters lived next door. Examines the gap between neighborhood appearances and disturbing realities.

title: Furthest Reaches of Space (Ed Reads Short Sci-fi, vol. VIII)
description: Wanderlust' by Alan E. Nourse Before the great journey comes the hope, the dream, the familial disappointment...and the truth. ‘The Sense of Wonder’ by Milton Lesser A generational ship far out in the stars...out so long, basic human concepts begin to change... ‘Mr Meek – Musketeer!’ by Clifford D. Simak After years of saving up for a ship, Meek is ready to retire. Until moral indignation sets in! ‘Wanderer of Infinity’ by Harl Vincent A science experiment opens a portal to a far off world and there they find the Wanderer... ‘X Marks the Asteroid’ by Ross Rocklynne A bold trip to Titan to find a hidden family secret. But can he trust the wiles of the beautiful and manipulative Fayette? Cross, double-cross, TRIPLE-cross!... ‘Blind Man’s Lantern’ by Allen Kim Lang An Amish couple travel through deep space to find a new homestead and come across other intrepid settlers with needs of their own. ‘Travelogue’ by Roger Dee A science fiction author, starved of ideas, receives a mysterious brochure in the post... ‘The Good Seed’ by Mack Reynolds A crash on a planet in deep space, and, staring death in the face, a traveller learns a profound truth about their way of life. ‘Pen Pal’ by Milton Lesser A MOST mysterious man, she finds in the local ads. Now to find him. And find out his story. ‘The Course of Logic’ by Lester del Rey Two travellers on a deep space mission find a strange new species, one intractably illogical. The name of the species? HUMAN. ‘Journey for the Brave’ by Alan E. Nourse To end the volume, it's the first man on the moon! And a deep dive into his fears... - Summary by The Reader

title: Weird Tales Presents: Web of the Spider!
description: My dear friends... Do you find that you suffer from the condition known as ARACHNOPHOBIA? Do you cower in fear at the feeling of those hairy little legs as they crawl slowly up your leg, a wisp of webbing in their wake? Do you spend sleepless nights shivering in horror at the idea of their sharp fangs sinking into your tender, soft flesh? Boy, do we have a collection of stories for you! From those madcap maniacs at Weird Tales magazine, we bring you eleven spine-tingling tales of arachnid terror that is sure to give you goosebumps! - Summary by Ben Tucker

title: Great Explorers in Space (Ed Reads Short Sci-fi, vol. I)
description: ‘The Pioneer’ by Irving E. Cox The greatest explorer of them all returns to Earth and finds the world upside down! ‘Flight Perilous’ by Ray C Noll A trip through the Asteroid Belt. They must make it to the other side. But will they? ‘Ask a Foolish Question’ by Robert Sheckley Douglas Adams foreshadowed. ‘The Vegans Were Curious’ by Winston Marks Alien explorers come to Earth to investigate radioactivity. They discover - cough – something else. ‘The Cosmic Snare’ by Milton Lesser A couple working the great gate of sub-space receive an unwanted guest. ‘Meeting at the Summit’ by Ivar Jorgenson Humankind en route to the stars. The United States President receives visitors with grave requisites. ‘The Voyage of Vanishing Men’ by Stanley Mullen The great unknown. But just what happened to Braun’s crew. Why did he come back alone? ‘Problem Planet’ by R. R. Winterbotham Three people alone. Two men argue over a woman. The best solution found. ‘The Last Plunge’ by S. J. Sackett What lies below the atmosphere of Uranus? What creatures lurk in its atmosphere? How can they be exploited for money? ‘Planet of Doom’ by Stephen Marlowe As a galactic reporter Jane Crowley knew she had hold of the biggest story of the year; but thousands of people were in peril. ‘Beware the Star Gods’ by S. J. Byrne Kuru stood his ground bravely as the ship flamed down from the sky. Truly this was a great and terrible moment. ‘Rocket Summer’ by Ray Bradbury The first great rocket flight into space, bearing intrepid pioneers to the Moon. The world's ecstasy flared into red mob-hate when President Stanley canceled the flight. How did he get that way? - Summary by Edmund Bloxam

title: Dastardly Criminals in Space (Ed Reads Short Sci-fi, vol. IV)
description: Conspiracy on Callisto - Frederik Pohl Revolt was flaring on Callisto, and Peter Duane held the secret that would make the uprising a success or failure. Yet he could make no move, could favor no side—his memory was gone—he didn't know for whom he fought! Prison of a Billion Years -CH Thames A prison that it is literally impossible to break free from? How could this be? Watchbird - Robert Sheckley Machines that can accurately predict when crimes will happen? What a great money spinner? But where's Tom Cruise when you need him? The Incomplete Theft - Ralph Burke A grand new spaceship, tech that could win the war for the thief's planet! Infiltrating the construction team leaves but one task... The Barbarians - Tom Godwin An intergalactic emperor finds dastardly HUMANS entering his space. What to do? Bleekman’s Planet - Ivar Jorgensen A retired cop wants a quiet retirement, so he chooses to emigrate to the most isolated inhabited planet of them all. Little does he know who's REALLY in charge down there... The Inquisitor - Randall Garrett A machine that can judge criminality? Well, if it's a machine, it MUST be flawless. Traitor’s Choice - Paul W Fairman A terrible dilemma: tech to destroy the world, or your wife. Hunting License - James V. McConnell We really ought to let the mega rich have their fun. And we can get rid of those nasty criminals at the same time. Morality? Nah. Bratton’s Idea - Manly Wade Wellman Hey, doll...wait, doll's don't talk...or shoot... - Summary by The Reader

title: Almighty Dollar in Space (Ed Reads Short Sci-fi, vol. VI)
description: $1000 a Plate' by Jack McKenty - When science meets tourism, surely only the Almighty Dollar can win out... 'Guaranteed - Forever!' by Frank M. Robinson -Manning had spent his life exposing mail order frauds. But Forsythe's outfit topped them all. Its products were too good to exist—yet! 'Mr Meek Plays Polo' by Clifford D. Simak - Does anyone know how to play SPACE polo? 'A Zloor For Your Trouble' by Mack Reynolds - It's an easy buck to catch a Martian rabbit. I wonder why the Government's paying so much... ‘Headhunters of Nuamerica’ by Stanton A Coblentz ‘No-Risk Planet’ by Milton Lesser - Oh, for a world without life insurance… Forsyte’s Retreat’ by Winston K. Marks - A down and out unemployed alcoholic lands on his feet working as a hotel manager. But why do people keep quitting the job? And just what is going on in time and space ‘Prospector’s Special’ by Robert Sheckley- He bet all his possessions, his relationships, his future, on one big horde in the deserts of Venus. Now he will bet his life... ‘The Luckiest Man in Denv’ by Simon Eisner - A stratified society! Cross! Double cross! ‘No Charge for Alterations’ by H. L. Gold - In a Control economy, it's vitally important that everyone does as they're told. If they don't, you change their mind. Literally. But who has the real power? ‘A Matter of Ethics’ by Russ Winterbotham - In honorable academic career or a vast payrise (with more fishing!). Oh, what of this matter of ethics to cloud his decision… ‘Fee of the Fronteir’ by H. B. Fyfe - At the final, pitiful colony on Mars, three bold explorers prepare for the great leap into the unknown. A trip to a casino is far from the highest payment they must be prepared to face. ‘4-1/2B, Eros’ by Malcolm Jameson - In the great trade routes of the solar system, corruption is rife! ‘The Plagiarist from Rigel IV’ by Evan Hunter - Well, this guy certainly didn't write 'Game of Thrones'. (He finished his stuff, for one thing). ‘Lex’ by W. T. Haggert - Imagine a factory that could run itself. Imagine the consequences. A 1950s look at what AI would be like. She Knew He Was Coming’ by Kris Neville - A tragedy in deep space. In the far future, men will still be men. ‘Synthetic Hero’ by Erik Fennel - A corporate leader so vilely selfish, he will lie and cheat his way to the top, sociopathically incapable of thinking of anyone but himself. But when he suddenly saves humanity, something must be amiss... ‘Never Gut-Shoot a Wampus’ by Winston K. Marks - When the rich can buy anything and anyone, when laws and ethics are irrelevant, to what level are they willing to go? ‘Superjoemulloy’ by Scott F. Grenville - How to be truly successful in business? Be self-important and mean, apparently. ‘You Too Can Be a Millionaire’ by Noel Loomis - Life had become a mad scramble for points.

title: Practice The Word
author: Practice The Word
description:
Practice the Word is a Christ-centered podcast for people who are ready to stop sitting on the sidelines of faith and start living the Word of God in everyday life.

Hosted by believers who love Jesus, serve people, and understand the real-world trials of business, family, ministry, hardship, and obedience, this show is for anyone who feels called to step out of the boat and walk with Christ more deeply.

Each episode encourages listeners to read Scripture for themselves, trust the Holy Spirit, serve others with sincerity, and become doers of the Word — not hearers only.

Through biblical conversations, personal stories, interviews with Christian leaders, and practical encouragement, Practice the Word exists to point people back to Jesus, remind them that every day is a gift from the Lord, and challenge them to live fully surrendered to Him.

Whether you are new to faith, returning to God, wrestling through a difficult season, or ready to go deeper, this podcast is an invitation to get off the fence, open the Bible, and practice the Word.

title: Entirely New Feature of a Thrilling Novel! Entitled, The Social War of the year 1900
description: Described by io9.com contributor Jess Nevins as “the worst science fiction novel of the 19th century,” The Social War of 1900, or The Conspirators and Lovers! has become infamous for its overwrought prose, flimsy characterizations, ludicrous plotting, and repugnant ideas. For these same reasons, the book has gained infamy as a cultural oddity, a guilty pleasure, and a somewhat over-imaginative work of outsider art. The Social War of 1900 follows the adventures of Dr. Victor Juno, a Naturalist who heals sickness through animal magnetism, and his sweetheart Lucinda. Juno, the leader of a secret paramilitary society, wages a revolutionary war against an American society steeped in sin and avarice—and, in particular, against a villainous triumvirate known as “The Bloody Conspirators.” But will his revolution succeed? And if so, what form will his idealized utopia finally take? - Summary by ChuckW

title: Fevers and Physicians in Space (Ed Reads Short Sci-fi, vol. II)
description: I: ‘Pandemic’ by Jesse F. Bone A deadly pandemic consumes the world (blah blah blah - you know the rest). If only THAT was the cure to the real one? II: ‘2BR02B’ by Kurt Vonnegut I wanna live forever...and have kids...but what about the consequences? III: ‘The Beautiful People’ by Charles Beaumont Why would you not want to be beautiful? Like every else. Now, now, don't be difficult. IV: ‘Operation Distress’ by Lester del Rey One man alone in the depths of space...suddenly struck with illness...if he returns to Earth, he could infect the world! V: ‘Doctor’ by Murray Leinster In the complex logistics of interstellar travel, the only way to cordon of the deadly plague is to ghettoize entire planets and let everyone die. Problem solved. But does a Zero-plague strategy really work? VI: ‘John’s Other Practice’ by Winston K. Marks Malpractice? Or will technology be allowed to innovate health care? Can Dr. Klinghammer convince the authorities that the corporation is not trying to scam them? VII: ‘Bolden’s Pets’ by F. L. Wallace I have a disease and all the planet's natives gave me was this lousy pet. But at least I have a cute pet now. Or do I? VIII: ‘The Addicts’ by William Morrison Another kind of disease. This time with a flawless and simple cure. But what if he won't take it? What if he's fatalistic about the strange creatures outside in space? IX: ‘The Man Who Made the World’ by Richard Matheson "Who is this strange patient I must see, nurse? He seems a bit of a brat." X: ‘Contagion’ by Katherine Maclean When colonizing a new planet, please remember: look out for new contagions, pay attention to the fact that your body can't digest the food (genetic manipulation required!) And beefcakes. Watch out for beefcakes. XI: ‘Breeder Reaction’ by Winston K Marks Finally, the medical scientists get hold of the new beauty product. The 'Mad Men' types try to cover up their sloppiness, and their bellies... XII: ‘The Disembodied Man’ by Larry Maddock A cure for death itself! It'll take some patience though. And a mysterious voice. XIII: ‘Advanced Chemistry’ by Jack G. Huekels No, it's actually a cure for death this time. For real. But accidents will happen... XIV: ‘Bad Medicine’ by Robert Sheckley A man intent on murder attempts a common therapy for psychosis. With disastrous results. XV: ‘Homesick’ by Lyn Venable Space, full of pathogens and allergens the human body can't cope with. Especially on...alien...planets. XVI: ‘Heart' by Henry Slesar "Money!" the big man cried. "How much money would it take to get me repaired?" "But it's not a matter of money—" "Don't give me that!" Monk put his jacket on with a violent motion. The physician shrugged. "Money doesn't buy health." "Doesn't it?" The patient gave an abrupt laugh. XIX: ‘The Happy Unfortunate’ by Robert Silverberg 'Healthy little ape'—those were the words he used. He told my grandmother I'd get bigger and bigger, that no amount of surgery could make me small and handsome, that I was fit only for space and didn't belong in Yawk. So I left for space the next morning. --Summary by The Reader

title: Seattle Executive Edge: A Podcast for Seattle Business Owners
author: Seattle Executive Association
description:
Go inside the relationships and stories driving Seattle business owners — from tradespeople and small business founders to tech leaders and executives.
Hosted by Seattle Executives Association, a century-old professional community meeting weekly at the Washington Athletic Club. 
Each episode features conversations with executives and entrepreneurs on trust, partnership, and how the right network changes everything.
 
Subscribe for real stories from the people building Seattle's business community and join us for a meeting. Learn more: https://www.seattleexecs.org

title: 2AM IN THE BRONX
author: Memphis
description: "2AM in the Bronx" shines a light on the raw, unpolished journey of rising creators and entrepreneurs before they reach the spotlight. Moving past surface-level success narratives, it breaks down the late-night risks, setbacks, and grind. This is an unfiltered look at the exact mindset and hard work required to turn a vision into reality.

title: Midnight Confession
description: People revealing their participation in unsolved crimes, covered-up incidents, or criminal conspiracies—sometimes seeking redemption, sometimes just unburdening themselves before death. Features confessions that reopened investigations, solved mysteries, or revealed crimes no one knew happened. Explores why people eventually tell secrets they've kept for decades and what happens when they do.

title: Labyrinth Versus Law Unlocking Crime Puzzles
description: As we probe deeper into the gritty bowels of the city, Labyrinth Versus Law dissects complex crime mysteries while exploring criminological theories. We unfold criminal motivations, tactics, and the relentless pursuit of justice through interviews with experts from the field of law enforcement and forensic science.

title: Voice of Courage
author: Tata Cancer Care Foundation
description: This is an Initiative by Tata Cancer Care Foundation to drive awareness about cancer care.

title: Justice Unzipped Exploring Urban Crime Anatomy
description: Step into the real-world maze of urban crime. This podcast pulls back the veil on hard-hitting investigations, criminal psychology, and the pursuit of justice in the darkest corners of city life. Join us as we unravel the complex threads of crimes that shook the city, with insights from law enforcement professionals, forensic specialists, and criminal justice experts.

title: In the Shadows Unraveling Crime Stories
description: A deep and intense exploration of the underworld, casting light on dark corners of urban life and revealing secrets only obtained through expert interviews and careful investigation.

title: Lost World (version 3)
description: The Lost World is a novel released in 1912 by Sir Arthur Conan Doyle about an expedition to a plateau in the Amazon basin in Brazil that encountered prehistoric animals. It has been the inspiration for subsequent fiction, including Michael Crichton's Jurassic Park.

title: The Mother Load
author: The Straits Times
description:
Synopsis: Join us for a five-part series on working motherhood: honest conversations and real stories about how women navigate careers, caregiving and the expectations that come with both.
This series isn’t just for mums – it’s for anyone trying to make sense of where careers and motherhood intersect. Through candid conversations and lived experiences, we unpack the issues shaping everyday decisions - from childcare and career progression to guilt, ambition and the invisible mental load.
New episodes every Thursday. 
Host: Vanessa Chelvan
Produced and edited by: Teo Tong Kai
Executive producers: Ernest Luis & Elizabeth Law
A podcast by The Straits Times, SPH Media.

title: 20 Short Science Fiction Stories
description: Compilation of 20 Science Fiction Short Stories - Summary by Kirk Ziegler

title: Description of Millenium Hall and the Country Adjacent
description: Utopian novel by Sarah Scott, published in 1762 describes women living in complete harmony. Summary by kathrinee

title: Astounding Stories 16, April 1931
description: Contents of this issue :-MONSTERS OF MARS EDMOND HAMILTON - Three Martian-Duped Earth-Men Swing Open the Gates of Space That for So Long Had Barred the Greedy Hordes of the Red Planet. (A Complete Novelette.) THE EXILE OF TIME RAY CUMMINGS - From Somewhere Out of Time Come a Swarm of Robots Who Inflict on New York the Awful Vengeance of the Diabolical Cripple Tugh. (Beginning a Four-Part Novel.) HELL'S DIMENSION TOM CURRY - Professor Lambert Deliberately Ventures into a Vibrational Dimension to Join His Fiancée in Its Magnetic Torture-Fields. THE WORLD BEHIND THE MOON PAUL ERNST - Two Intrepid Earth-Men Fight It Out with the Horrific Monsters of Zeud's Frightful Jungles. FOUR MILES WITHIN ANTHONY GILMORE - Far Down into the Earth Goes a Gleaming Metal Sphere Whose Passengers Are Deadly Enemies. (A Complete Novelette.) THE LAKE OF LIGHT JACK WILLIAMSON - In the Frozen Wastes at the Bottom of the World Two Explorers Find a Strange Pool of White Fire—and Have a Strange Adventure. THE GHOST WORLD SEWELL PEASLEE WRIGHT - Commander John Hanson Records Another of His Thrilling Interplanetary Adventures with the Special Patrol Service. THE READERS' CORNER ALL OF US - A Meeting Place for Readers of Astounding Stories.

title: Insurance Fraud Investigators
description: Private investigators expose elaborate insurance scams—from staged accidents to fake injuries to arson-for-profit schemes. This podcast follows experienced detectives as they surveil suspects, gather evidence, and prove that 'victims' are actually criminals defrauding insurance companies for millions. Listeners will be fascinated by the creativity of insurance fraud and the dogged detective work that exposes even sophisticated schemes.

title: Astounding Stories 06, June 1930
description: Issue six of this seminal science-fiction magazine concludes the Ray Cummings story "Brigands of the Moon", and continues Murray Leinster's "Murder Madness". In addition there are three short stories, by various authors, and a short novel by Charles W. Diffin - Summary by Alan Winterrowd

title: Astounding Stories 15, March 1931
description: This issue includes "When the Mountain Came to Miramar" by Charles W. Diffin, "Beyond the Vanishing Point" by Ray Cummings, "Terrors Unseen" by Harl Vincent, the conclusion of "Phalanxes of Atlans" by F. V. W. Mason, and "The Meteor Girl" by Jack Williamson. - Summary by Bill Boerst

title: Astounding Stories 10, October 1930
description: Issue no. 10 of the magazine brings you:- Stolen Brains by Captain S.P. MeekThe Invisible Death by Victor Rousseau Prisoners on the Electron by Robert H. Leitfred Part 2 of Jetta of the Lowlands by Ray Cummings An Extra Man by Jackson Gee along with the Readers' Corner and interesting scientific facts (Summary by Annise)

title: Appliance Repair Lexington: Tips, Tricks & Fixes
author: Shellina Calimlim
description:
Welcome to the Appliance Repair Services Lexington podcast, your ultimate audio guide to keeping your household running smoothly! Whether you’re dealing with a refrigerator that won't cool, a noisy washing machine, or a malfunctioning oven, we’ve got you covered.

On this show, we share expert maintenance tips, straightforward DIY troubleshooting advice, and insider secrets to extend the lifespan of your major home appliances. Proudly serving the Lexington, Kentucky community, our mission is to help you save time, avoid costly breakdowns, and know exactly when to call in the professionals.

For trusted, reliable local service and expert support, visit " https://lexingtonappliancerepairky.com/ ". Tune in each week to master your home maintenance and keep your appliances performing like new!

title: Warlord of Kor
description: Terry Carr never really shone as a writer, though he did write some remarkably thoughtful stories. However, his talents as an editor and anthologist were important and undeniable, and he brought many good writers and authors into sf and fantasy. His first couple of novels, of which Warlord of Kor was one, did not have their copyright renewed by Mr. Carr on purpose, one understands. So enjoy the blood and thunder, but don't think it's representative of his work! (Summary by suburbanbanshee)

title: Premium Sandbox
author: PLACEHOLDER
description: Premium Sandbox

title: Harsh Metro: Anatomy of Urban Chaos
description: In 'Harsh Metro', we delve into the intricate labyrinth of urban chaos, bridging the gap between law enforcement and the public. This podcast features riveting interviews, gripping case studies, and expert insights on urban crime, criminal psychology, and the judicial pursuit.

title: Cue for Quiet
description: After too many years, T. L. Sherred returns with a story that gets our SPACE SPECIAL rating. It's the story of a man with a headache—who found a cure for it! And the cure gave him more power than any man could dream of. - Summary by Space Science Fiction May 1953

title: Grit of the Streets Exploring Urban Crime
description: This podcast takes you on an absorbing journey into criminal psychology, true crime analysis, and the raw reality of urban life, offering an unfiltered look into the grit of city streets.

title: Newshound
description: The Fourth Estate was highly specialized in the 22nd Century; for example, a good newsman predicted coming events—and made them happen.... - Summary by Imagination Stories of Science and Fantasy July 1955

title: Status Civilization
description: Will Barrent awakes without memories just before being deposited on Omega, a planet for criminals where the average life expectancy is 3 years. He’s listed as a murderer and released into the illicit society as a “peon” the lowest class imaginable. A mysterious girl gives him a weapon that starts him on his path to status, a path that requires constant brutality. But it must be borne if our hero is to discover the reason for his imprisonment; A reason that pits him against himself, and involves the sardonically similar but devoutly different creeds of Omega and Earth. - The Status Civilization was first published as “Omega!” in the August and September 1960 issues of Amazing Science Fiction Stories Magazine. (Summary by Gregg Margarite)

title: Space Prison
description: AFTER TWO CENTURIES....The sound came swiftly nearer, rising in pitch and swelling in volume. Then it broke through the clouds, tall and black and beautifully deadly — the Gern battle cruiser, come to seek them out and destroy them. Humbolt dropped inside the stockade, exulting. For two hundred years his people had been waiting for the chance to fight the mighty Gern Empire ... with bows and arrows against blasters and bombs! (Summary from the Gutenberg text)

title: FRANK.
author: Steve Bisley
description: The Best Stories.

title: Weird Fiction of Fantasy Fan Magazine
description: The Fantasy Fan was a fanzine edited by Charles D. Hornig that featured exclusive works by many seminal figures from the golden period of weird fiction including H. P. Lovecraft, Robert E. Howard, R. H. Barlow, Robert Bloch and many others. This collection seeks to compile together all the fiction published by The Fantasy Fan during its two-year run, excluding Clark Ashton Smith whose Fantasy Fan works have previously been published in this collection: Fantasy Fan - Summary by Ben Tucker

title: When the World Screamed
description: A new Professor Challenger Story! When Mr. Peerless Jones, artesian borer, is contact by the great but eccentric Professor Challenger with an urgent request, his curiosity and the endorsement of his friend Malone who had previous dealings with Challenger convinces him to meet with the Professor. Challenger's plan is as absurd as it is potentially Earth-shattering... Will Jones experience the sound of WHEN THE WORLD SCREAMED? - Summary by Ben Tucker

title: Relaxing Scottish Meditations
author: Richard Mason
description: Each new podcast will gift you a hypnotic body scan to lull you into a trance, then transport you to a location in Scotland where you will relax in the stories and history of this ancient kingdom.

title: Poison Belt
description: Three years after the events that took place in The Lost World ( http://librivox.org/the-lost-w... ), Professor Challenger urgently summons his fellow explorers (Professor Summerlee, Lord John Roxton, and reporter E.D. Malone) to a meeting. Oddly, he requires each to bring an oxygen cylinder with him.What he soon informs them is that from astronomical data and just-received telegraphs of strange accidents on the other side of the world, he has deduced that the Earth is starting to move through a region of space containing something poisonous to humankind.Shutting themselves tightly up in Challenger's house, they start to consider what may be done. But as their countrymen start to drop, will their oxygen last long enough to determine and implement a solution? (Summary by Mark Smith)

title: Astounding Stories 05, May 1930
description: The fifth issue of Astounding Stories continues the Ray Cummings serial "Brigands of the Moon", begins a 4 part novel by Murray Leinster, and presents a sequel by Sewell Peaslee Wright to his story in the March issue. Also included are stories by Lilith Lorraine and Victor Rousseau. - Summary by Alan Winterrowd

title: Astounding Stories 11, November 1930
description: This science-fiction mag includes "The Wall of Death" by Victor Rousseau, "The Pirate Planet" by Charles W. Diffin, "The Destroyer" by William Merriam Rouse, "The Gray Plague" by L. A. Eshbach, "Jetta of the Lowlands" by Ray Cummings, and "Vagabonds of Space" by Harl Vincent. Summary by Bill Boerst

title: The New P&L: Patterns & Leaders
author: Michael Winn & Kryssi Murrell
description: The traditional P&L tells you what happened in a business. The New P&L explores why. Through authentic conversations about leadership, growth, trust, accountability, and culture, we uncover the human patterns that drive organizational success. Because behind every business result is a story of people. Hosted by Kryssi Murrell and Michael Winn. Presented by K&K Accounting Services. Powered by RBOA - Digitally Driven Marketing. Learn more at www.rboa.com/the-new-pl-podcast/

title: Green Splotches
description: IF any one were to ask us for a perfect example of the scientifiction story, we would perhaps unhesitatingly say "The Green Splotches." Here is a story that mixes science with fiction to the nth degree. It is a story that will persist in your memory for many years. You can never forget it. Then, also, you will get more out of it by reading it a second and third time than you did the first time. With each reading the story will improve, and it will give you more food for thought. Here, then, is the scientifiction story par excellence, and we know in advance that when you fill in your voting coupon, this story will rank first. - Summary by Amazing Stories, March 1927

title: Astounding Stories 01, January 1930
description: In January of 1930 a new magazine with a flashy color cover appeared on newsstands, Astounding Stories of Super-Science. Filled with stories of adventure, sometimes with only a tinge of science, this magazine was to host and nurture many science fiction giants like Murray Leinster and Ray Cummings and would help inspire many of the writers of the "Golden Age of Science Fiction". This inaugural issue includes stories by Murray Leinster, Ray Cummings, S. P. Meek, Victor Rousseau and others. (Summary by Alan Winterrowd)

title: Slaves to the Metal Horde
description: Johnny Hope knew the robot armies had been created to serve Man. But war and a plague had destroyed civilization, leaving humans as - slaves to the metal horde! - Summary by Original Gutenberg text

title: Collected Public Domain Works of Stanley G. Weinbaum
description: Stanley G. Weinbaum is best known for his short story “A Martian Odyssey” which has been influencing Science Fiction since it was first published in 1934. Weinbaum is considered the first writer to contrive an alien who thought as well as a human, but not like a human. A Martian Odyssey and its sequel are presented here as well as other Weinbaum gems including 3 stories featuring the egomaniacal physicist Haskel van Manderpootz and his former student, playboy Dixon Wells. (Summary by Gregg Margarite)

title: Recruit for Andromeda
description: No one comes back from the Nowhere Journey, whatever they say about some “rotation.” When Kit Temple's drafted, he figures he's leaving his girl, his life, behind forever. But his fellow-draftee, the enigmatic Arkalion, seems to know a lot more than what he's letting on. When Kit follows Arkalion to the end of space and time, he finds out what's really going on in this “Nowhere” and takes on a trial that will decide the fate of Earth itself! (Summary by Andrew Huguelet)

title: Martian Odyssey & A Valley of Dreams
description: The first of these stories was originally published in the July 1934 issue of Wonder Stories. It was followed four months later by a sequel, "Valley of Dreams" in the same magazine. These classic stories take us to Mars where we meet a Martian, or at least something very different from us, and several other completely original specimens of life. The Martian "Tweel" looks like an ostrich and the Egyptian god Osiris - for good reason, as you will find out if you listen to the story! These are both classic, funny, strange, entertaining, and, in my opinion, great stories by an imaginative master - summary by philc

title: Astounding Stories 17, May 1931
description: This issue of the science-fiction magazine includes a novella by Charles W. Diffin titled "Dark Moon" and several short stories, including "When Caverns Yawned", by Captain S.P. Meek "When the Moon Turned Green", by Hal K. Wells and "The Death-Cloud." by Nat Schachner & Arthur L. Zagat There is also a second part of "The Exile of Time" by Ray Cummings. - Summary by Bill Boerst

title: Maracot Deep
description: Professor Maracot, accompanied by two American associates, conducts an exploration of the Atlantic Ocean floor, beginning in a diving bell of his invention. The results exceed the underwater thrills we may recall from Sea Hunt and Jacques Cousteau. Maracot, a contrastive successor to Professor Challenger, whom Edgar Rice Burrows had borrowed from Doyle, leads his colleagues into unanticipated adventures that rival those of Burrows's Barsoom novels. These climax with a supernatural contest of wills. (Summary by Thomas A. Copeland)

title: Nine Sci-fi Stories by Poul Anderson
description: Industrial Revolution': Workers in a distant miner's facility plot emancipation from Earth. The military might have some say in the matter. 'Captive of the Centaurianess': A scientist, a warrior princess and a Martian walk into a bar. And revolutionise space travel. 'What Shall it Profit?' A mysterious, underground research facility is discovered. Does it hold the key to eternal life? Or much deeper consequences? 'The Virgin of Valkarion': In a Rome-like empire, a barbarian encounters a warlord with a scheme to overthrow the empire, and a princess that wants to stop it. 'Catalysis': Mutiny brews on a long-distance spaceship. Are they all going to die? 'World of the Mad': A scientist's wife keeps having terrifying visions. Is it the planet or is it just her? 'Innocent at Large' (Written with Karen Anderson): When attempting to scam an experienced deep space explorer, one must be careful. 'Witch of the Demon Seas': A warrior with a vanquished army. Across the dangerous seas to reclaim his fame! 'Sargasso of Lost Starships': A general with a vanquished army. Into the dangerous void to reclaim his fame! (Summary by the reader)

title: Vampires of Space
description: Commander John Hanson recounts his harrowing adventure with the Electites of space. Beings of Ether who don't want your blood, they want your soul and eat your entire ship along with it. Listen as Commander Hanson and his men face certain death but battle their way out as only the men of the intrepid Space Patrol can. - Summary by phil chenevert

title: His Wisdom, the Defender: A Story
description: A millionaire genius invents a flying armored suit, a la Iron Man. - Summary by Terry Kroenung

title: Tarrano the Conqueror
description: In "Tarrano the Conqueror" is presented a tale of the year 2430 A.D.--a time somewhat farther beyond our present-day era than we are beyond Columbus' discovery of America. My desire has been to create for you the impression that you have suddenly been plunged forward into that time--to give you the feeling Columbus might have had could he have read a novel of our present-day life. To this end I have conceived myself a writer of that future time, addressing his contemporary public. You are to imagine yourself reading a present day translation of my original text--a translation so free that a thousand little colloquialisms will have crept into it that could not possibly have their counterparts in the year 2430. - Summary by From the author's preface

title: Grit & Guilt: Unveiling Urban Crime
description: Grit & Guilt delves into the underlying narratives of urban crime, criminal psychology, and justice pursuits. With features including detailed case analyses, interviews with experts, and discussions on the lust for power and money that often drive these crimes, this podcast is a must-listen for true crime enthusiasts.

title: Girl in the Golden Atom
description: While examining a golden ring under a microscope, a chemist discovers a sub-atomic world. During his examination of this world he sees a beautiful young girl. After developing chemicals that will allow him to either shrink or grow larger in size, he and three friends journey to this small world. (Summary by Richard Kilmer)

title: Happy Unfortunate
description: Here are two early stories by the well known SF Author Robert Silverberg. The Happy Unfortunate was published first in Amazing Stories in 1957 and explores the angst caused when the human race reaches into space but at the cost of needing to breed a new species; specialized 'spacers' who can withstand the tremendous rigors of acceleration. The Hunted Heroes was published in Amazing stories a year earlier, in 1956. It is a futuristic story that holds great hope for the resilience of the human race after the war destroys most of the world.

title: Jewels of Aptor
description: Delany's first novel, from 1962, serves as a sort of prologue to the subsequent Captives of the Flame, 1963. Set several centuries after the Great Fire -- a nuclear holocaust -- a young woman seeks her destiny with the help of a four-armed youth. (Summary by BellonaTimes)

title: Captives of the Flame
description: Chip Delany's 2nd novel -- the first is The Jewels of Aptor (1962) -- published by Ace Books in 1963. Set in the 35th Century, the survivors of a nuclear war live on the coastline and an island in a kingdom ruled by a royal family in disrepair. A young victim -- the son of a wealthy merchant -- of their wrath becomes a working-class hero as he fights to get back his good name, aided by a disaffected member of the royal family. This was later rewritten as Out of The Dead City by Delany as part of the Towers Trilogy, an early masterpiece, IMO. (Introduction by BellonaTimes)

title: Erewhon
description: Erewhon, or Over the Range is a novel by Samuel Butler, published anonymously in 1872. The title is also the name of a country, supposedly discovered by the protagonist. In the novel, it is not revealed in which part of the world Erewhon is, but it is clear that it is a fictional country. Butler meant the title to be read as the word Nowhere backwards, even though the letters "h" and "w" are transposed. It is likely that he did this to protect himself from accusations of being unpatriotic, although Erewhon is obviously a satire of Victorian society. (summary from Wikipedia)

title: Two Tales about Aviation
description: Two science fiction stories in which Rudyard Kipling imagined a future world controlled by the Aërial Board of Control with its motto, "Transportation is Civilization". In 'With the Night Mail' a postal packet makes an eventful night run across the North Atlantic from London to Quebec..... . A follow up story 'As Easy as ABC' had more to say about the political organization of this new world. (Summary by Phil Benson)- Summary by Phil Benson

title: What Not
